questions.question: Why does the Moon revolve around the Earth?
questions.answer: Due to the gravitational attraction between Earth and Moon.

questions.question: What happens to gravitational force if masses of both objects double?
questions.answer: The gravitational force becomes four times.

questions.question: What happens to gravitational force if distance is doubled?
questions.answer: The force becomes one-fourth.

questions.question: Why is gravitational force called universal?
questions.answer: Because it acts between all objects in the universe.

questions.question: Why is gravitational force considered weak?
questions.answer: Because it becomes noticeable only when masses are very large.

questions.question: Why do astronauts feel weightless in space?
questions.answer: Because they are in continuous free fall.

questions.question: What is apparent weight?
questions.answer: The reduced weight of an object when immersed in fluid.

questions.question: Why does a sheet of paper fall slower than a stone?
questions.answer: Because air resistance affects the paper more.

questions.question: Why are knives made sharp?
questions.answer: To increase pressure by reducing area.

questions.question: Why do army tanks have wide tracks?
questions.answer: To reduce pressure on the ground.

questions.question: Why do objects weigh less in water?
questions.answer: Because buoyant force acts upward.

questions.question: What determines whether an object floats or sinks?
questions.answer: The density of the object compared with the fluid.

questions.question: Why do large ships float despite being heavy?
questions.answer: Because the buoyant force balances their weight.

questions.question: Why is pressure greater on a small area?
questions.answer: Because pressure is inversely proportional to area.

questions.question: Why do we feel lighter while swimming?
questions.answer: Due to buoyant force exerted by water.

questions.question: Why does a balloon rise in air?
questions.answer: Because buoyant force of air is greater than its weight.

questions.question: What happens when buoyant force equals weight?
questions.answer: The object floats.

questions.question: Why does g decrease with altitude?
questions.answer: Because distance from Earth's center increases.

questions.question: Why does g vary at poles and equator?
questions.answer: Due to Earth's shape and rotation.

questions.question: What happens to weight if mass increases?
questions.answer: Weight increases proportionally.

questions.question: Why are hydrometers used?
questions.answer: To measure the density of liquids.

questions.question: Why are lactometers used?
questions.answer: To measure the purity of milk.

questions.question: Why do submarines adjust ballast tanks?
questions.answer: To control buoyancy.

questions.question: Why does a heavy ship float but a small iron nail sinks?
questions.answer: Because ships displace more water creating greater buoyant force.

questions.question: Why is Archimedes' principle important?
questions.answer: It explains floating and sinking of objects in fluids.
